# howznguyen-content

> Content repository for howznguyen.dev. Blog posts and project showcases as MDX files, fetched at runtime by the main Next.js site.

This file follows the [llms.txt](https://llmstxt.org/) convention. Instructions for AI agents (Cursor, Copilot, Claude) helping to author MDX content.

## Key facts

- Content-only repo. No application code.
- Consumed by howznguyen.dev via GitHub Contents API.
- Preview tool: https://howznguyen.dev/preview
- Push to `main` triggers a webhook that revalidates the site within seconds.

## Repository layout

```
posts/                       Blog posts (.mdx)
projects/                    Project showcases (.mdx)
assets/                      Optional committed images
README.md
AGENTS.md
llms.txt
```

Rules:
- Only `.mdx` files in `posts/` and `projects/` are published.
- Files starting with `_` are ignored (for drafts and templates).
- No subdirectories inside `posts/` or `projects/`.

## Filename ŌåÆ URL

- `posts/my-post.mdx` ŌåÆ `/blog/my-post` (neutral, no language tag)
- `posts/my-post.en.mdx` ŌåÆ `/blog/my-post/en`
- `posts/my-post.vi.mdx` ŌåÆ `/blog/my-post/vi`
- `projects/my-project.mdx` ŌåÆ `/projects/my-project`

## Translations

Posts and projects can be translated by adding a locale suffix before `.mdx`.

- A file **without a suffix** is "neutral" ŌĆö not tagged with any language. Good for code-heavy or reference content.
- A file **with a suffix** is a specific translation (e.g. `.en`, `.vi`, `.ja`).
- A post may have only neutral, only localized, or both.
- If there is no neutral file, the base URL serves the first available translation (prefers `en`).
- The language switcher appears automatically when more than one variant exists.

Supported locale codes (ISO 639-1): `en`, `vi`, `ja`, `zh`, `ko`, `fr`, `es`, `de`, `pt`, `ru`.

## Frontmatter

### Posts (`posts/*.mdx`)

```yaml
---
title: string                  # required
description: string            # required, shown on cards + meta tags
date: YYYY-MM-DD               # required
updatedAt: YYYY-MM-DD          # optional
tags: [string]                 # required, first tag is primary category
author: string                 # optional, single GitHub username (no @)
authors: [string]              # optional, multiple GitHub usernames
featured: boolean              # optional
image: url                     # optional, cover image URL
---
```

### Projects (`projects/*.mdx`)

```yaml
---
title: string                  # required
description: string            # required
date: YYYY-MM-DD               # required
tags: [string]                 # required

# Classification (affects badge + gradient + icon)
type: "open-source" | "side-project" | "work" | "experiment" | "product" | "writing"
status: "active" | "wip" | "shipped" | "archived" | "sunset"

# Work-specific
role: string                   # e.g. "Founding Engineer"
company: string                # e.g. "Knowns Dev"
year: number | string

featured: boolean
image: url                     # optional
images: [url]                  # optional gallery (3824├Ś2474 recommended)
github: url                    # optional
demo: url                      # optional
authors: [string]              # optional GitHub usernames
---
```

### Project type and status

| type           | icon | for                                                  |
|----------------|------|------------------------------------------------------|
| `open-source`  | ŌŁÉ   | OSS libraries, CLIs, tools                           |
| `side-project` | ­¤Ä©   | Personal hobby projects (default)                    |
| `work`         | ­¤Æ╝   | Client or company work                               |
| `experiment`   | ­¤¦¬   | Prototypes, experiments                              |
| `product`      | ­¤ÜĆ   | Launched SaaS or commercial products                 |
| `writing`      | ­¤ōØ   | Docs, guides, manuscripts                            |

Status options: `active` (pulsing green), `wip` (pulsing blue), `shipped` (green), `archived` (gray), `sunset` (orange).

### Authors

- `author: "<github-username>"` for a single author.
- `authors: ["user1", "user2"]` for multiple.
- Both fields can coexist; the site dedupes preserving order.
- Leading `@` is stripped. Usernames must match `[A-Za-z0-9-]{1,39}`.
- Display names (with spaces) in `author` are treated as invalid and fall back to the site owner.

## Markdown features

Standard markdown: `#`ŌĆō`####` headings, `**bold**`, `*italic*`, `inline code`, `~~strikethrough~~`, ordered/unordered/task lists, tables, blockquotes, links, images, horizontal rules.

GFM extensions enabled: tables, task lists, strikethrough, autolinks.

## Code blocks

```typescript
const greeting = "Hello"
```

Languages: `typescript`, `ts`, `javascript`, `js`, `tsx`, `jsx`, `python`, `py`, `go`, `rust`, `rs`, `bash`, `sh`, `shell`, `zsh`, `json`, `yaml`, `yml`, `html`, `css`, `scss`, `sql`, `graphql`, `dockerfile`, `nginx`, `caddyfile`, `toml`, `md`, `mdx`, `text`.

## Custom components

Auto-injected in every MDX file. Do NOT `import` them.

### `<Callout>`
```mdx
<Callout type="info" title="Heads up">
  Content supports **markdown**.
</Callout>
```
- `type`: `info` (default) | `warning` | `error` | `success` | `note` | `tip`
- `title`: optional, defaults to the type name
- `emoji`: optional override (default per type: ­¤ÆĪ ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ ­¤Ü© Ō£ģ ­¤ōØ Ō£©)

### `<Image>`
```mdx
<Image src="https://..." alt="..." caption="..." />
```
Click-to-zoom enabled. `alt` is required for accessibility.

### `<Bookmark>`
```mdx
<Bookmark url="https://..." title="..." description="..." />
```
Props: `url` (required), `title`, `description`, `icon`, `cover`.

### `<Video>`
```mdx
<Video src="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ID" />
```
YouTube URLs auto-detected. Other URLs use native `<video controls>`.

### `<TodoList>` + `<Todo>`
```mdx
<TodoList>
  <Todo checked>Done item</Todo>
  <Todo>Pending item</Todo>
</TodoList>
```
GFM task lists (`- [x] item`) also work.

### `<Quote>`
```mdx
<Quote color="blue">Memorable quote.</Quote>
```
`color`: `gray` (default) | `red` | `blue` | `green` | `yellow` | `orange` | `purple`.

### `<Code>`
```mdx
<Code language="typescript" title="src/utils.ts">
{`export const hello = "world";`}
</Code>
```
Prefer fenced code blocks; use `<Code>` when you need a custom title.

## Images

1. **External CDN (preferred):** Cloudinary, Imgur, S3 ŌĆö paste URL.
2. **Commit to `assets/`** and reference via jsDelivr:
   ```
   https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/howznguyen/howznguyen-content@main/assets/posts/file.png
   ```

## Publishing workflow

1. Fork the repo.
2. Copy `posts/_template.mdx` to `posts/your-slug.mdx` (add `.en` / `.vi` etc. for translations).
3. Write the post following the frontmatter and component contract above.
4. Preview at https://howznguyen.dev/preview.
5. Open a pull request against `main`.

## Style guide

- `description`: Ōēż 160 characters for best social previews.
- `date`: always `YYYY-MM-DD`.
- `tags`: 2ŌĆō5 tags per post. First becomes the category label.
- Body headings start at `##` (H1 reserved for the rendered title).
- Code blocks: always specify a language tag.
- Self-close MDX components with no children: `<Video src="..." />`.

## Things to avoid

- Do not add imports in MDX.
- Do not use components other than the ones listed above.
- Do not put `.mdx` files in subdirectories.
- Do not commit assets > 1MB; use a CDN.
- Do not use display names in the `author` field ŌĆö use GitHub usernames.

## Related

- Main site: https://howznguyen.dev
- Preview tool: https://howznguyen.dev/preview
- This repo: https://github.com/howznguyen/howznguyen-content
